title: Pin NumPy below 2.0 for CANN toolkit compatibility
problem_description: NumPy 2.0 removed the `np.float_` alias (replaced by `np.float64`), but CANN toolkit's internal Python code (in `te`, `auto_tune`, and HCCL parser modules) still references `np.float_`. When a clean venv installs the latest NumPy (2.2.x), CANN initialization fails at import time with `AttributeError: module 'numpy' has no attribute 'float_'`. This silently blocks all NPU operations.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Detect CANN toolkit version (from `/usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/latest/version.cfg`). 2. Before installing project dependencies, pin NumPy: `pip install 'numpy<2.0'` (specifically 1.26.4 for CANN 8.1.RC1). 3. If project's requirements.txt pulls in a later NumPy, install pinned version after: `pip install numpy==1.26.4 --force-reinstall`. 4. Verify: `python -c 'import numpy; print(numpy.__version__)'` shows 1.x.
